name: La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop
name_original: Ruta de La Muela – trail run circular desde El Puertecito
description: This difficult 5.2-mile (8.4 km) circular trail run from El Puertecito offers panoramic views and a challenging workout in Sierra de Béjar.
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Garganta offer a diverse environment for outdoor activities, characterized by river valleys, gorges, and mountainous terrain. The region features impressive ravines with natural pools, dense forests, and hilly landscapes. Converted historical routes, such as greenways, provide relatively flat and well-maintained surfaces suitable for running. Altitudes range from lower valleys to higher mountain areas, offering varied challenges and scenery. Best jogging routes around La Garganta The most popular jogging route is Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata. Another top favourite among local runners is Cancho La Muela via the Turbal de Navamuño – circular trail run in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space, a difficult 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail offers a challenging run through the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space. Local runners also love the La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around La Garganta is defined by river valleys, dense forests, and mountainous landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more difficult trail runs. The routes in La Garganta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore La Garganta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Garganta?
A: La Garganta offers a wide selection of over 60 running routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging trail runs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around La Garganta?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain, including river valleys, dense forests, and hilly to mountainous landscapes. Some routes follow converted historical paths like greenways, offering relatively flat and well-maintained surfaces, while others ascend to higher mountain areas with more challenging conditions.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Garganta?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in La Garganta are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993696841/via-de-la-plata-penacaballera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 6.3-mile (10.2 km) run.

Q: What are some challenging trail runs in the region?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993687289/cancho-la-muela-via-the-turbal-de-navamuno-circular-trail-run-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho La Muela via the Turbal de Navamuño – circular trail run in the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space&lt;/a&gt; provide a difficult 6.2-mile (10.0 km) path with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993683968/pena-negra-via-the-navamuno-reservoir-trail-run-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra via the Navamuño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 7-mile (11.2 km) trail.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by natural pools or waterfalls?
A: While specific running routes may not directly pass through all natural pools, the region is famous for its natural features. The La Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, for instance, features impressive ravines with 13 natural pools, often called "marmitas de gigante." You can find beautiful waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinajarro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area, which can be explored in conjunction with your runs.

Q: What kind of views or landmarks can I see while running in La Garganta?
A: Running in La Garganta offers panoramic views of canyons, rocky cliffs, and surrounding mountains. You might encounter historical elements like sections of the ancient Vía de la Plata, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993696841/via-de-la-plata-penacaballera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features notable viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and impressive mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/904739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Garganta?
A: The running routes in La Garganta are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars from numerous reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from scenic river valleys to challenging mountain paths, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running paths in La Garganta?
A: Yes, there are options suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier run. While many routes involve hilly terrain, some converted greenways offer relatively flat and well-maintained surfaces. You can find at least one easy route among the 69 available, with many moderate options also being accessible for those new to trail running.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, some routes provide scenic views of local water bodies. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Baños – Hervás Old Town loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque vistas of the Embalse de Baños reservoir, combining natural beauty with a touch of local history.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in La Garganta?
A: La Garganta offers appealing conditions for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter can bring snowy mountain landscapes, especially at higher altitudes, the lower valleys and greenways remain accessible, offering a different kind of beauty. Summers are ideal for enjoying the refreshing natural pools after a run.

Q: Are there any historical routes suitable for jogging in La Garganta?
A: Absolutely. The region features converted historical routes, such as sections of the ancient Vía de la Plata. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993696841/via-de-la-plata-penacaballera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, allowing you to run along a path with rich historical significance through the Corpus de Hombre river valley.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Sierra de Candelario Natural Space?
A: Yes, several challenging routes take you through the stunning Sierra de Candelario Natural Space. These include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993687289/cancho-la-muela-via-the-turbal-de-navamuno-circular-trail-run-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho La Muela via the Turbal de Navamuño – circular trail run&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993683968/pena-negra-via-the-navamuno-reservoir-trail-run-in-the-sierra-de-candelario-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra via the Navamuño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering immersive experiences in this protected area.
tips: Pico Cancho La Muela, located between La Garganta (Cáceres) and the Sierra de Béjar, very close to Hervás: Spectacular views of the Hoya Moros Glacial Cirque, the Sierra de Béjar, the Ambroz Valley, the Sierra de Gata, the Sierra de Francia and the Navamuño reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Ruta de La Muela trail run?
faqs.answer: This circular trail run is rated as difficult. While some 'Ruta de La Muela' trails can be moderate, this specific route involves significant elevation changes and varied terrain, making it best suited for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and can be challenging. Expect unpaved paths, potentially rocky sections, and steep ascents and descents. Appropriate sports shoes with good grip are essential to navigate the varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers beautiful panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. You will pass by Cancho La Muela, a summit that provides expansive vistas. Depending on the specific path, you might also encounter diverse natural features and potentially historical elements.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta de La Muela trail from El Puertecito?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact trail starting from El Puertecito is not readily available. However, for trails of this nature, it's common to find parking near the main access points of the village or designated trailheads. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival or consulting local tourism resources for the most up-to-date parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de La Muela trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas like this are dog-friendly, but specific regulations can vary. If dogs are permitted, they should always be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please check local park rules upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail largely depends on local climate. To avoid extreme heat or cold, spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially for potential rain or high winds.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the trail or Sierra de Béjar?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Sierra de Béjar. While many natural parks in Spain do not require specific permits for hiking or trail running on established routes, it's always wise to confirm. There are typically no entrance fees for accessing the general park area, but specific activities or protected zones might have different rules. We advise checking the official Sierra de Béjar park website for any current regulations regarding permits or fees.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find parts of the [CIMA CC12] La Garganta (Collado de las Angosturas) * Baños de Montemayor and the [CIMA CC12] La Garganta (Collado de las Angosturas) * Hervás routes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, this specific Ruta de La Muela trail run is generally not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of fitness and experience in trail running. Easier, more family-friendly options might be available in the broader region.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near El Puertecito?
faqs.answer: El Puertecito is a small village, so amenities might be limited directly at the trailhead. However, nearby towns or larger villages in the Sierra de Béjar region typically offer a range of services including cafes, local restaurants, and various accommodation options. It's advisable to plan ahead and check for services in the surrounding area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on Ruta de La Muela?
faqs.answer: For this difficult trail, essential items include appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water and snacks, a map or GPS device (like the komoot app) for navigation, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit is also highly recommended.
